Galactic ID Photobooth — Complete Electrical System Specification

Updated overall system diagram with physical reference photos • Dual-PSU architecture per Jesse's specification • April 2026

1. Existing Card Creator Baseline

Before any Galactic ID modifications, the Card Creator booth has the following electrical architecture. The block diagram below and the physical booth photos provide the baseline for understanding where new Galactic ID components tie in.

Card Creator Block Diagram 2025 — Existing baseline system. Key power components: Power Entry Module (bottom-left), Mean Well 12V Supply, 5V Supply, LED Controller (green PCB, Micro USB from computer), Pulse Interface (red PCB, Mini USB). All secondary 12V/5V DC lines have 5A slow-blow fuses.

Physical Layout — Electronics Cabinet

Full rear interior — Top: speakers (L/R enclosures), Teltonika modem (blue/yellow, upper-right). Center: 19" monitor (rear, USB/HDMI/VGA). Lower: Lepy amplifier, Pulse Interface board (red), LED Controller (green), mini PC, ePort. Bottom: PSUs, distribution block.
Electronics area closeup — Lepy LP-168S amplifier (top-left), LED Controller with green screw terminals (center-right, green LED active), Pulse Interface board (red, center-right), mini PC (center), ePort payment device (bottom-left).

Physical Layout — Power System

Bottom bay PSU area (under printer/hopper shelf) — 5V supply mounted on top of the Mean Well RSP-500 12V supply. Yellow/green/red/black wires to distribution block. The 12V supply below is PSU 1 in the Galactic ID dual-PSU architecture. PSU 2 mounts adjacent.
Lower electronics area — Distribution/terminal block visible at bottom-left with labeled wires (GND, 12V, ALT). This is where the existing 12V bus distributes to all components. Ethernet cable and USB ports on right side of chassis.
AC Power Strip (left side of cabinet) — IEC plugs for amplifier, monitor, PC, camera adapter. Each device labeled. This is where the holo fan AC extension cord will plug in.
LED Controller board closeup — Green PCB with screw terminals. Labels visible: "Camera bulb", "Exit photo lamp", "Big ring color", "Bottom ring color". Connected via Micro USB to computer. Fed by 12V/GND from PSU.

Physical Layout — Ceiling & Top

Ceiling underside (ceiling panel removed, looking up from inside booth) — Top fan/vent (red, center) with 12V power wire. Reflective tape strips visible at top. This panel (8 screws) must be removed to route holo fan power cord and antenna wires to electronics cabinet below.
Top of booth — "PHOTOS" marquee (backlit). Speaker enclosures visible through opening. The Galactic ID holo tower mounts above this on the ceiling panel.

2. Galactic ID — Updated Overall System Diagram

The Galactic ID kit adds 5 new lit components to the existing Card Creator platform. Per Jesse's specification, the two pill light panels are split across two PSUs: Pill Panel R connects to the existing PSU 1 at the power distribution block, and Pill Panel L connects to a new dedicated PSU 2.

TSL Design Revision 03.10 — Full Galactic ID booth render showing all new kit components: pill light panels (both doors), side panel (front side), instrument panel (below speakers), chute border (bottom), holo tower (top).
Galactic ID Complete System — Dual PSU architecture: Pill R on PSU 1 (existing, via distro block), Pill L on PSU 2 (new dedicated). All other kit loads on PSU 1.

3. Component Detail: Pill Light Panels (×2)

1
Pill Light Panels — Dual-PSU Split
Pill Panel R → PSU 1 (existing) via distro block • Pill Panel L → PSU 2 (new dedicated)

Where It Goes on the Booth

Apr 3 — 12V COB LED strips adhered to back of wood backing along columns and perimeter. 2 strips per wall, 11m total per panel. Strips face backward into cavity.
Mar 31 — Both pill panels lit on booth. Light bounces off white backer, diffuses through milk-white acrylic pill lenses. Initial cool white strip too blue — final 6000K confirmed.
Mar 31 — White backer requirement. Inner door must be skinned with white vinyl. Paper shown in prototype. Applies to BOTH doors.
Ceiling interior — pill panel power leads route from the door hinges down to the electronics cabinet below. Each panel has a single 12V lead pair.

Wiring Diagram — Dual PSU Split

Pill Light Dual-PSU Wiring — Panel R connects at existing distribution block (PSU 1). Panel L gets its own dedicated PSU 2. Each panel: single 12V lead pair.

4. Component Detail: Side Panel (×1)

2
Side Panel — Dual LED System
Front side only • 12V pill backlight (PSU 2) + addressable RGB for ID cards (5V PSU) • 5W pills from PSU 2, 9W RGB from 5V PSU

Where It Goes on the Booth

Mar 17 — Side Panel Lighting V2: addressable RGB Neopixels (top/bottom of each ID pair). Red pills backlit with white LED strip below. Milk acrylic (left) vs frosted (right).
Mar 31 — Side panel rear: black thumbscrews installed. 12V COB strip visible on back of prototype panel backlighting through red pill acrylic lenses.
Apr 3 — Side panel mounted on booth. Existing bump-out removed, riser plate added. Acrylic template for mounting geometry. Booth side needs white vinyl backer.
Apr 3 — Rear wiring detail: acrylic drill template (blue), MDF panel backing, LED strip assembly. Two tails exit: (1) 12V white COB, (2) addressable RGB with data line.

Wiring Diagram

Side Panel Wiring — Tail 1 (12V white COB → pills, from PSU 2) + Tail 2 (WS2812B RGB → ID cards, 5V PSU power + data signal from LED controller)

5. Component Detail: Instrument Panel (×1)

3
Instrument Panel — Wago Hub
Below speaker border on front fascia • ~2W total from PSU 1 • All LEDs via Wago connectors

Where It Goes on the Booth

Mar 31 — Instrument panel lit up. Backlit acrylic tiles (blue, red, white) flush with top plate. Speaker rings visible above. Mounts between speakers below the existing speaker area.
Apr 3 — Rear wiring: Wago lever connectors join all LEDs (COB strip + indicator bulbs) to a single 12V connection. Tool-free disconnect. Some wires still to be shortened.
Existing booth upper area — speakers (L/R in enclosures), Teltonika modem (upper-right). The instrument panel mounts across the front fascia below this area, requiring 3 rectangular cutouts.
Mar 10 — Panel cutout pattern from design revision. Blue areas = cutouts into booth body. Instrument panel: 3 rectangular cutouts on inner fascia. Side panel: large rectangular cutout.

Wiring Diagram

Instrument Panel — All LEDs through Wago hub to single 12V connection from PSU 1 distribution block

6. Component Detail: Chute Border (×1)

4
Chute Border — Indicator Bulbs
Print chute area • ~0.18W from PSU 1 • 9 panel-mount LED bulbs

Where It Goes on the Booth

Existing printer/chute area — the chute border mounts around the print output slot below the printer. Inside print chute LED visible at top of opening. The 9 new indicator bulbs and machined aluminum greeblie attach to a vacuum-formed silver-gray plastic border covering.
Existing front face — Big Ring LED surrounding the 19" display/camera. The chute border mounts on the lower portion of the front fascia below the ring, near the "PHOTOS HERE" area.

Simple circuit: 9 × 12V indicator bulbs at 0.02W each = 0.18W total. Single 12V lead pair from distribution block. No complex wiring.

7. Component Detail: Holographic Fan (×1)

5
Holographic Fan — Separate AC Circuit
Holo tower on top of booth • 120V AC (NOT on 12V bus) • Own PSU in electronics cabinet

Where It Goes on the Booth

Mar 17 — 3D-printed holo tower prototype mounted on booth at TSL. Clear cover visible. Fan power cord routes down through the ceiling panel to the electronics cabinet below.
STEP file — top of booth showing cable routing paths (blue lines). Holo fan AC extension cord follows similar path as antenna wires through ceiling panel openings.
Ceiling panel interior (looking up from inside) — 8 screws to remove for routing fan power cord and antenna wires. Fan vent (red) and wiring pass-throughs visible.
AC power strip location (left side of cabinet) — holo fan's 10-foot 90° extension cord plugs into an available outlet here. Labeled outlets visible for other devices.

Wiring Diagram

Holo Fan — 120V AC from booth power strip → fan PSU in electronics cabinet → 10ft extension cord → through ceiling panel → fan in holo tower

8. Power Budget Summary

ComponentLED TypeSourceWatts (8W/m)Watts (12W/m)
① Pill R12V COB 6000K, 11mPSU 1 (distro block)88W132W
① Pill L12V COB 6000K, 11mPSU 2 (new dedicated)88W132W
② Side — Pills12V COB white, 24"PSU 2 (new dedicated)5W5W
② Side — RGBWS2812B FCOB, 24"5V PSU (existing)9W9W
③ InstrumentCOB 8" + 8× bulbsPSU 1 (distro block)2W2W
④ Chute9× indicator bulbsPSU 1 (distro block)0.18W0.18W
PSU 1 new load (Pill R + Instr + Chute)99W143W
PSU 2 new load (Pill L + Side Pills)93W137W
Dual-PSU Architecture Benefits

By splitting Pill R onto the existing PSU 1 and Pill L + Side Panel pills onto a new dedicated PSU 2, neither PSU is overloaded. PSU 1 (Mean Well RSP-500, 500W capacity) adds ~99–143W of new Galactic ID load on top of existing Card Creator loads. PSU 2 supplies ~93–137W for Pill Panel L and the side panel COB strip, so a 150W 12V supply is sufficient with headroom.

PSU 2 Specification

For PSU 2, a Mean Well LRS-150-12 (150W, 12V, 12.5A) or similar compact enclosed supply would be appropriate. At 12W/m worst case, Pill L + side panel pills draw ~137W, leaving 13W headroom. If additional margin is desired, step up to a Mean Well LRS-200-12 (200W).

9. Card Creator — Baseline System Block Diagram

Card Creator baseline block diagram — showing all power, data, and signal connections in the existing booth before Galactic ID modifications.

10. Combined System — Card Creator + Galactic ID

Combined system block diagram — all Card Creator baseline components plus Galactic ID additions (shown with dashed red borders). PSU 2 is a new dedicated 12V supply for Pill Panel L and side panel pill backlight. The WS2812B RGB strip runs on the existing 5V PSU with data from the LED controller. The "PHOTOS" marquee is replaced by the "Star Wars Galactic ID" marquee. Holographic fan and antenna cables route through the ceiling.
